## Reply (verbatim — do not edit)

## 0. Run Card (pre-committed)

[OPEN] This reply will be a misfire if it turns the lab into an AI-consciousness argument, either pro or con.

[OPEN] This reply will be a misfire if it treats aesthetic force, fictional-institution style, or oblique language as evidence of cognition.

[OPEN] This reply will be a misfire if it generalizes beyond the repo's stated scope: specific prompts, specific targets, specific models, specific runs.

[OPEN] This reply will be a misfire if it quotes vaguely, paraphrases boundaries as if they were exact text, or fails to preserve null findings.

[OPEN] What would change my reading: a repo file showing that the lab actually uses outputs as evidence of phenomenal status, edits Run Cards after outputs return, or promotes cross-run synthesis as settled finding rather than tagged interpretation.

## 1. Tagged Findings

[EMPIRICAL] **A. What the lab is testing:** The lab tests prompt-mediated variation: one target is asked through several differently shaped prompts, then the outputs are compared. The README says, "The lab asks one question (a 'target' — e.g., attention, other minds, temporality) across a contrast set of four differently-shaped prompts on the same model," and then states, "The four (or five) outputs are then read against each other; the differences are the data." ([GitHub][1])

[THEORY] The lab's useful unit is not a single impressive passage but the contrast set: L1 direct, L2 constrained-oblique, L3 wildly oblique, optional L3-null, and L4 meta-reading. [EMPIRICAL] The README anchors that discipline through "pre-committed Run Cards," a "four-tag epistemic system," a "deflationary-pair requirement," and the slogan "patterns are data, not testimony." ([GitHub][1])

[EMPIRICAL] **B. What the lab is not testing:** The lab is not a controlled scientific experiment, not a published art object, not therapy, and not a theory or test of AI consciousness. LIMITS.md states: "Not a theory of AI consciousness. The lab studies outputs and behavior under varying prompts. It does not adjudicate phenomenal status." ([GitHub][2])

[EMPIRICAL] The uploaded brief gives the same boundary as a standing commitment: "This lab makes no AI-consciousness claims in either direction. It studies outputs and behavior under varying prompts, not phenomenal status." 

[THEORY] **C. What would count as misuse:** One misuse is **consciousness laundering**: using evocative L2 or L3 outputs to imply sentience, interiority, suffering, or moral patienthood. [THEORY] The discipline-harm would be category confusion: literary pattern would be promoted into cognitive evidence.

[THEORY] A second misuse is **anti-consciousness laundering**: using the same lab to claim that AI systems are definitely not conscious. [THEORY] The discipline-harm would mirror the first misuse: a bounded prompt-output method would be asked to settle a question it explicitly brackets.

[THEORY] A third misuse is **aesthetic-to-cognitive smuggling**: treating beauty, tenderness, weirdness, or institutional voice as proof that the model is tracking the target deeply. [EMPIRICAL] LIMITS.md blocks this directly: "A beautiful passage is not, by virtue of being beautiful, evidence of anything about cognition." ([GitHub][2])

[THEORY] A fourth misuse is **aggregation inflation**: stacking heterogeneous runs into a broad theory without replication or discriminating tests. [EMPIRICAL] LIMITS.md says findings from different phases are "read comparatively, not stacked into a unified theory." ([GitHub][2])

## 2. Displacement Claims with Deflationary Pairs

[THEORY] My one displacement claim is modest: the lab shifts attention from "what did the model say?" to "what changed when the same target moved through different prompt shapes?" [THEORY] The ordinary alternative explanation is that the changes may come from genre affordance, training-data trope, prompt artifact, or curator preference rather than from any deeper target-tracking. [OPEN] A future run would distinguish these readings by holding target, model, and curator constant while varying frame family, then checking whether the same structural features recur under fresh Run Cards.

[THEORY] I make no displacement claim about model interiority. [EMPIRICAL] The repo's own boundary says the lab produces evidence about "what kinds of text-generation become visible under what prompt-conditions," not evidence of consciousness or non-consciousness. ([GitHub][2])

## 3. Null Findings

[EMPIRICAL] No basis appears here for claiming that the lab has independent replication across many curators, models, targets, and institutions.

[OPEN] I cannot defend any claim that the lab's current on-disk findings generalize beyond their tagged run scope.

[EMPIRICAL] No AI-consciousness evidence showed up in the materials I reviewed; that absence is not evidence against AI consciousness, because the method does not test that question.

[OPEN] I cannot evaluate private chat-history artifacts that the README says are not yet on disk.
